summary: This is an extremely brief post consisting of a single sentence quoting Earthlink's response about aiming for an 'effect of verisimilitude,' with a link to an external source. The post is too short to contain a substantive argument or conclusion. It appears to be a quick link-blog entry, common in early 2000s blogging, where Joel shared an amusing or noteworthy quote with minimal commentary.
key_points: A one-line link-blog entry sharing an Earthlink quote about 'verisimilitude'
Reflects the early blogging style of short, link-driven posts with little original commentary
money_quotes: We're shooting for an 'effect of verisimilitude'.
key_insight: This micro-post is a link-blog artifact from early 2000s blogging culture, too brief to convey a substantive insight.
